methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Pragmatic Semiconductor is a Series D-stage semiconductor company that has raised £182M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 4 private companies in the same sector at the same or adjacent stage: PsiQuantum (Series E, $7.0B), SambaNova Systems (Series D, $5.0B), Tenstorrent (Series D, $2.7B), and DEEPX (Series C, $529M).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable approach. The median valuation across these four comparables is $3.9B, with a range spanning $529M to $7.0B. This reflects the substantial variance in valuations within the frontier-chip space at Series D and adjacent stages. Public-company anchoring via P/S multiples could not be applied, as Pragmatic's revenue is not disclosed—a limitation typical of private semiconductor firms. The public Semiconductors sector (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) trades at a median 24.4x P/S, but without the target's top-line figure, this method yields no quantitative output.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $3.3B to $4.4B, with a median of $3.9B. Key uncertainties include the maturity of Pragmatic's product roadmap relative to comparables, actual revenue run rate, and capital efficiency relative to peers. The wide peer dispersion ($529M–$7.0B) reflects heterogeneous business models and commercialization stages within the sector.
